creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a decorator-based framework for deploying GPU workloads directly from an IDE with hot reload capabilities. It simplifies the deployment cycle by replacing Docker builds and manual GPU allocation with a single @flash.endpoint decorator on Python functions. RunPod provides H100 GPU access at $0.00116 per second with serverless autoscaling options.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but rather misunderstood. The real problem was a narrow definition of retrieval, which should encompass vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively. Cursor's measured gains from semantic search and rising Google search volume contradict the "RAG is dead" narrative.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview processes audio with speaker labels, timestamps, emotion tags, and language detection in a single API call. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is a sound-to-sound real-time multimodal model with integrated reasoning that handles both speech understanding and generation directed by natural language prompts.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel is promoting the World's Fair event scheduled for San Francisco from June 29 to July 2, 2026. Tickets and schedule information are available at ai.engineer/wf.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream on Saturday to discuss AI coding, agents, and related tools. The stream will cover topics including Claude Code and Archon while the host plays chess simultaneously.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that routes frontend development tasks to different LLMs: Claude Opus for planning and reasoning, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI generation, and back to Opus for integration. The approach uses handoff documents to structure communication between agents and leverages tools like Pi and Archon to orchestrate the pipeline.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: A workflow that uses Claude Opus for planning and integration, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design, and orchestrates them through Archon to build beautiful frontends efficiently. The approach leverages each model's strengths in sequence: Opus for logic and copy, Gemini for design quality, then Opus again for integrations and deployment.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering separates the wrapping infrastructure around AI coding agents from the model itself, treating the harness as the critical component for reliability and scale. It operates at two levels: the AI layer managing rules, skills, hooks, and subagents within a session, and orchestration across multiple focused agent sessions to handle larger tasks reliably.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video tutorial on building proactive AI agents that autonomously optimize processes and self-improve. Covers proactive AI loops, autonomous ad optimization and SEO use cases, and tools like Loopany that enable agent memory and tool integration.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding capabilities, covering setup, prompting strategies, and multi-step task execution through /Mission exploration. The video suggests autonomous coding tooling is maturing toward practical deployment.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason presents OpenAI Symphony as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, harness setup with auto testing, and WORKFLOW.md configuration for using Symphony in projects.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews recent advances in agent systems, covering autoresearch and autogen capabilities, Claude's memory architecture, and self-evolving mechanisms in frameworks like Hermes and Openclaw. The video discusses enabling technologies and comparative design approaches for agentic AI systems.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 introduces MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) to replace full attention, shifting the model architecture. The implementation includes tiled KV cache optimizations and I/O improvements to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages at inference time.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework that provides a harness layer for building and deploying AI agents. The video compares Pi's approach to frontier agent systems like Claude Code, Cursor, and others, examining how it handles complexity as more products add agent features.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of a Tau Scaling Law designed to compete in the global semiconductor race against the USA. The video examines the role of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding as key technologies driving the industry forward amid China-USA competition.

videos.title: Agent Harness explained in 8min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents agent harness engineering as an evolving discipline that extends beyond prompt and context engineering to better control agentic systems. The video traces the progression from prompt engineering through context engineering and harness engineering, addressing limitations and architectural considerations of modern agents.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: How to Beat AI in Your Dev Job Search
videos.summary: AWS Developers video argues that networking and personal branding are more effective than mass resume applications in tech job searches when AI screens candidates. It frames AI as a labor market disruptor that evolves roles rather than eliminating them, and recommends hands-on learning through open source and SaaS projects for junior developers.

videos.title: Host any coding agent on AgentCore
videos.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables hosting and running multiple coding agents including Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Kiro. The service provides a unified platform for deploying various coding agents on AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: Five production deployment patterns for AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsiveness, layered authentication (API Gateway, Cognito, IAM), type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost controls and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. Applicable across frameworks despite AWS focus.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A tutorial video on using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API by defining infrastructure as code. The video compares manual AWS console setup against CDK automation and covers DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway deployment.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: How Meta AI accidentally gave away Obama's instagram
videos.summary: Meta's AI system reportedly identified Barack Obama's Instagram account when prompted, potentially exposing personal information through an AI tool. The incident highlights privacy and security risks in AI systems deployed at scale.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube discussion comparing code generation practices at Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The video examines what counts as "vibing code" and contrasts approaches across companies from 2024 to 2026 projections.

videos.title: CEO finds out tokens cost money
videos.summary: A YouTube video by Alberta Tech presenting a comedic or critical take on the realization that LLM token usage incurs costs. The title suggests commentary on business decision-makers discovering the financial implications of deploying large language models.

videos.title: He's a marketing "engineer"!? 🤨
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech questions someone's use of the "engineer" title in a marketing context. The skeptical tone suggests debate over professional credential authenticity or job title inflation.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that rabid users do not signal real product-market fit and that founders often mistake passion for a small market segment as PMF. True PMF manifests as demand pull rather than push, and should be assessed through embedded customer relationships rather than formal interviews. He advocates betting on where AI models are heading, not just their current state.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization rather than subscriptions is the path forward for AI tools, and that the primary bottleneck for AI agents is human trust adoption rather than technical capability. He raised $26M by applying probabilistic decision-making frameworks from competitive sailing to startup investment strategy.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, discusses his bootstrapping journey to $10M ARR without external funding. The interview covers strategies for achieving $1M ARR milestones, reducing churn, product-led growth decisions, SEO and outbound tactics, pricing experiments, and when to consider raising capital.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh advocates teaching AI through manual, foundational approaches rather than via tools and frameworks. He argues that deep mathematical understanding survives industry shifts and hype cycles better than tool-dependent skills.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5: The New AI That Changes Everything! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof promotes Claude Fable 5.0 as a distinct AI model separate from Opus, highlighting a feature called Know Your Agent (KYA). The video claims the model has transformative impact on the AI landscape.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof reviews Anthropic's newly announced Claude Fable 5 model, discussing its capabilities for regulated industries and potential use cases. The video covers context engineering for AI agents and design patterns for long-running applications.

videos.title: Unlock AI Agent Control: Gemma 4, Local Power & Your Questions Answer #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses local control capabilities with Gemma 4 and solicits questions from the AI builder community for future content. The video emphasizes enabling local deployment and user control of AI models.

videos.title: AI is Going LOCAL! Edge AI & Local Agents Explained #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses the rise of edge AI and local agents as a solution for data-sensitive industries like finance and healthcare. It emphasizes the security and privacy advantages of running AI locally rather than in centralized cloud environments.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
